title: celtis iguanaea
intro: Celtis iguanaea, also known as the Mexican hackberry, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of the Celtis iguanaea plant have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Celtis iguanaea have been associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an antidote for various poisonings. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
